verdict.answer_first: B states the bottom line in sentence 1: "A database index is...to speed up lookups"; A starts with definition too but delays the tradeoff until later: "The cost..."
verdict.decisiveness: A gives the clearest single prescription: "Don't guess — measure" and "start with no indexes beyond your keys...then index deliberately"; B also says "Don't guess — measure" but ends with a broader checklist.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives a concrete decision loop: "Run EXPLAIN...Add it, re-run EXPLAIN, and confirm" plus specific traps like "LOWER(email)" and "LIKE '%smith'".
